While attending a fundraiser in May 1992, Kennedy and Bessette crossed paths again.

Kennedy's friend Sasha Chermayeff recalled the encounter in the book, saying, "Eventually I spotted Carolyn. Just by looking at her I realized he has brought me here to see her, to tacitly approve... There was something captivating about the way she moved, the tilt of her head. Carolyn was captivating. Without John even looking her way, my eyes went to her, and I knew."
Per Beller’s book, Kennedy and Bessette dated for a few months before breaking up temporarily.
The Couple Dated in 1994 and Got Engaged in 1995
The couple reconnected in 1994 and started dating again. However, they had a major obstacle to confront: Kennedy wouldn't introduce Bessette to his mother.
Per Beller's book, Bessette broke up with Kennedy "a couple of times" for not introducing her to his mother, Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis.
Eventually, their relationship settled down and Kennedy proposed in 1995. Three weeks later, Bessette accepted his proposal, per “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
The Couple Got Married in 1996
Bessette and Kennedy tied the knot in an intimate ceremony on Cumberland Island, a small island off the coast of Georgia, in September 1996.
They died in a plane crash three years later.

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Author Rachel Reid Addresses If ‘Unrivaled’ Is the Last Book About Shane and Ilya
summary: “Heated Rivalry” author Rachel Reid addresses if she has plans for more books about Shane and Ilya after "Unrivaled," and if she know show their story ends.
story: “Heated Rivalry” fans are waiting more chapters in Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov’s relationship — and the new pages might not be the last.
Rachel Reid, author of “Heated Rivalry” and the “Game Changers” hockey romance series, joined Jenna Bush Hager on a bonus podcast episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” out Feb. 12.

Steamy and heartfelt, Reid's book was adapted for the screen into a six-episode series. “Heated Rivalry” sparked a phenomenon and launched actors Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams to fame when it premiered on HBO Max in November.

Then in January, Reid announced Shane and Ilya’s love story would continue on the page in her upcoming book, “Unrivaled,” which marks the third book about the couple and the seventh book in the “Game Changers” series.
When asked if “Unrivaled” would be the final book about Shane and Ilya, Reid left the door open.
“I don’t want to say last,” she told Jenna. “You know, I thought ‘The Long Game’ was the last book, so you never know.”
That said, Reid is keeping a satisfying ending in mind as she finishes up “Unrivaled.”
“I try to write these books as if they could be the last book,” Reid said. “So I do try to wrap things up — but you know, you never know.”

“Unrivaled,” out Sept. 29, follows "Heated Rivalry's" sequel, “The Long Game." When Shane and Ilya’s relationship is accidentally exposed, the former rivals get to be in a public relationship for the first time. According to the synopsis, Shane and Ilya are enjoying married life and playing on the same hockey team, but backlash to their relationship in the sports world is brewing.
As she writes "Unrivaled," Jenna asked if Reid has an ending in mind for Shane and Ilya.
“I have in my head an idea of where they are, say, 10 years later,” Reid said.
However, Reid said she is not disclosing if she will complete that timeline in “Unrivaled.”

Meanwhile, the show is operating on a different timeline. Season 1 followed the events of Reid's first book about Shane and Ilya, and the already-renewed Season 2 is expected to incorporate the plot of “The Long Game.”
While Reid couldn’t outright confirm a Season 2 premiere date, she said of a spring 2027 return, “That is what I believe to be true.”
Jenna, on her end, is excited for more.
“Can’t wait for ‘Unrivaled,'" Jenna said. "We can’t wait for Season 2, and we’re just so happy for you — that people are falling in love with this world you’ve created and these characters."
Listen to the full "Open Book with Jenna" episode featuring Rachel Reid here.

title: James Van Der Beek Said Cancer Diagnosis Taught Him He Was 'Worthy of Love' In Final TODAY Interview
summary: In his final interview with TODAY's Craig Melvin, James Van Der Beek shared what his cancer diagnosis taught him, how his family helped him and the biggest takeway.
story: In his final interview with TODAY, James Van Der Beek said he had gained a new perspective on his life after being diagnosed with cancer.
The “Dawson’s Creek” star died Feb. 11. His wife, Kimberly Van Der Beek, shared the news in a joint Instagram. He was 48.

The actor had been diagnosed with colorectal cancer in 2023, and publicly shared the news in November 2024. At the time, he said that he had been privately dealing with his diagnosis and was “taking steps to resolve it, with the support of my incredible family.”
“There’s reason for optimism, and I’m feeling good,” he added at the time.
A year later, Van Der Beek sat down with TODAY’s Craig Melvin for an exclusive interview that aired Dec. 19, where he shared that his cancer diagnosis taught him he was “worthy of love.”
“The biggest change I’d say would be this journey of self love,” he said. “What I realized was, I’m still worthy of love. My own love, God’s love.”

The “Varsity Blues” star had undergone a colonoscopy after feeling like something was off with his body and experiencing symptoms that included a change in bowel habits. The procedure led to his cancer diagnosis.
Upon hearing his diagnosis, Van Der Beek recalled being in shock, but also thinking the news was the “best thing” that ever happened to him.

“I had this little voice in my head that said, ‘You’re going to make changes in your life that you would never, ever make if you didn’t have this extreme of a diagnosis,” he said. “And it’s going to add healthy, happy years to your life.”
The actor also shared that his diagnosis made him start eating healthier and slow down. He also said that it was "overwhelming" to see how much he had been showered with love.

“(To) have so many people tell me, “I’m praying for you. I’m wishing you the best. How you doin?’” he said. “I never expected that. I never just stopped to appreciate what I’d done. And I will say, every time somebody says, “I’m praying for you, and I’m wishing you the best,’ I appreciate it.”
Van Der Beek leaves behind six children — Olivia, Joshua, Annabel, Emilia, Gwendolyn and Jeremiah — ranging from 15 to 4 years old, whom he shares with Kimberly Van Der Beek.
In his final interview with TODAY, he told Craig that he “wouldn’t be alive” if it weren’t for his family.

“She just has stepped up as a caretaker, as a nurse, as head of the household,” he said. “As much as I want to shield them from them seeing me in any kind of pain or distress — I wanted to be Superman, Super Dad — you realize that resilience is not only something they have and can build, but it’s the best thing for them.
“I watch them deal with it, and I watch them handle situations I would never want to put them in,” he added. “And I watch them come out stronger and better people.”
Thanking everyone for their “outpouring support,” he added, “If anybody takes anything away from this interview, it would be get tested, talk to your doctor.”
CORRECTION (2/11 at 5:08): This article originally stated that James Van Der Beek shared his diagnosis in December 2024. It was November 2024.

title: ‘Dawson’s Creek’ Cast Pays Tribute to James Van Der Beek
summary: The cast of “Dawson’s Creek” are celebrating James Van Der Beek’s legacy following the news of the actor’s death at the age of 48.
story: The cast of “Dawson’s Creek” are celebrating James Van Der Beek’s legacy following the news of the actor’s death at the age of 48.
On Wednesday, Feb. 11, the “Varsity Blues” star’s wife, Kimberly Van Der Beek, wrote in an Instagram post that her husband had died.

“Our beloved James David Van Der Beek passed peacefully this morning,” she said in a joint post that included James Van Der Beek’s Instagram account. “He met his final days with courage, faith, and grace. There is much to share regarding his wishes, love for humanity and the sacredness of time. Those days will come. For now we ask for peaceful privacy as we grieve our loving husband, father, son, brother, and friend.”

James Van Der Beek publicly revealed that he had been diagnosed with colorectal cancer on Nov. 3, 2024. “I’ve been privately dealing with this diagnosis and have been taking steps to resolve it, with the support of my incredible family,” he said in a message.

After the post announcing his death was uploaded, many of James Van Der Beek’s co-stars throughout the years paid tribute to him, including the actors who shared the screen with him on “Dawson’s Creek.”
Katie Holmes posted a handwritten note to Instagram Feb. 12, writing in her caption, "I formed some words with a heavy heart."
Holmes played Van Der Beek's character's best friend and longtime love interest Joey Potter, who appeared in all six seasons of the teen drama.
She opened her note by simply writing, "Thank you," to Van Der Beek.

"I mourn this loss with a heart holding the reality of his absence and deep gratitude for his imprint on it," she added. "To Kimberly and the children, we are here for you always. And will always be there to shower you with love and compassion."
Busy Philipps, who played Audrey Liddell, shared throwback photos from their time on set together.

“My heart is deeply hurting for all of us today…every person who knew James and loved him, anyone who loved his work or had the pleasure of meeting him, all of his dear friends and community that surrounded him as he battled this illness, especially his parents and brother and sister,” she wrote. “But I am profoundly heartbroken for his incredible wife Kimberly and their six magical children.”
She mentioned the family’s GoFundMe page and said she would link to it in her Instagram story.

“James Van Der Beek was one in a billion and he will be forever missed and i don’t know what else to say,” she added. “I am just so so sad. He was my friend and i loved him and i’m so grateful for our friendship all these years.”

Chad Michael Murray, who briefly portrayed Charlie Todd for 12 episodes, commented beneath the Instagram post about James Van Der Beek’s death and said, “Sending love and light to your beautiful family. James was a giant. We’re so so so sorry for what you’re going through. His words, art and humanity inspired all of us — he inspired us to be better in all ways. God bless you guys.”

“Dawson’s Creek aired for six seasons on The WB from 1998 to 2003. In September, the cast reunited for a one-night only event in New York City. James Van Der Beek canceled the day before due to “two stomach viruses.”
He sent a surprise pre-recorded message instead to express his gratitude to the everyone who contributed to the show, including the fans.
“From the cast to the crew to everybody who’s doing anything and has been so generous, and especially every single last one of you,” he shared. “You are the best fans in the world.”

title: 'Love Is Blind' Creator Explains Why Season 10 Cast Members Victor and Christine Weren't Invited to Mexico
summary: Why didn't Vic and Christine go to Mexico in Season 10 of Love Is Blind? Creator Chris Coelen explains in a statement given to TODAY.com.
story: After seven couples got engaged in the "Love Is Blind" Season 10 dating pods, viewers were curious to know why only six of them vacationed together in Mexico, while another couple traveled to a different location. Now the show is sharing answers.

If you’ve already binge-watched the first few episodes of Season 10, you might've thought that Vic and Christine were a shoo-in for the romantic Mexican getaway, especially since they got engaged in the first episode.
However, the newly engaged couple didn't join their co-stars in Mexico. Instead, they went on a private trip to Malibu without cameras following them.
In a statement to TODAY.com, "Love Is Blind" executive producer Chris Coelen shared the following explanation.

“We’ve never had the resources to send more than six couples to Mexico, but this season, we felt so good about the couples that for the very first time we chose to follow seven of them, producing the biggest season in 'Love Is Blind' history! In order to make this work, we had to offer one couple the chance to go to Malibu on their own getaway," Coelen said.

One of the reasons that Vic and Christine were chosen to go on their own getaway was related to the fact that they didn’t have any unfinished business with other cast members from their time in the dating pods.

"While the romantic getaways are a chance to not only see if the couples can turn their emotional connection into a physical one, they are also a chance to address outstanding feelings and issues with others they may have dated in the pods," the statement continues.
"Christine and Vic had fewer intersecting relationships with the other remaining couples, so we offered them the chance to go to Malibu, and we were absolutely thrilled that they accepted."
Vic and Christine will appear throughout the remainder of the season since the production team started filming them once again when they returned to Ohio from their vacation.
"We’re so excited for all of our couples, and are delighted that we can share all of their stories with the world," the statement reads.

In an interview with Entertainment Weekly, "Love Is Blind" creator Chris Coelen explained how Christine and Vic responded when they learned that they would be traveling to another location, separate from the rest of the cast.

"It was exactly the reaction that you would want, which is they just were in love and felt like they wanted to spend time together," he said. "And I don’t think it really mattered to them whether they were going to go off and have time in Malibu or Mexico. They just really wanted to spend time with each other. And when I explained to them the reason they were going to send them to Malibu before sending them back to Ohio, they thought that it made total sense."
Viewers had mixed reactions to the unexpected plot twist.
"Someone tell me why vik and Christine didn’t go to Mexico? What am I missing ❤️," one confused viewer commented on Instagram.
Another kept their response short and sweet, writing: "Vic and christine 💖."

One Instagram user applauded Netflix's decision to give the couple their own vacation. "I just want a season of Vic & Christine. Happy they skipped out on Cabo to do their own thing! 🩵," they commented.

title: James Van Der Beek Dies at 48
summary: James Van Der Beek, the actor who rose to fame playing the teenage wannabe filmmaker Dawson Leery on the coming-of-age television drama “Dawson’s Creek,” has died
story: James Van Der Beek, the actor who rose to fame playing the teenage wannabe filmmaker Dawson Leery on the coming-of-age television drama “Dawson’s Creek,” has died.
His wife, Kimberly Van Der Beek, shared the news on Instagram Feb. 11.

“Our beloved James David Van Der Beek passed peacefully this morning,” she wrote in a post also shared to Van Der Beek’s Instagram. “He met his final days with courage, faith, and grace.  There is much to share regarding his wishes, love for humanity and the sacredness of time.  Those days will come.  For now we ask for peaceful privacy as we grieve our loving husband, father, son, brother, and friend.”
His representatives also confirmed his death with NBC News.

“Dawson’s Creek” aired on The WB from 1998-2003 and followed a group of friends through their teenage and young adult years. The series also starred Katie Holmes, Joshua Jackson and Michelle Williams.

Van Der Beek went on to play a fictional version of himself in the cult ABC sitcom “Don’t Trust the B---- in Apartment 23,” which aired 2012–2013. He later played FBI Agent Elijah Mundo on “CSI: Cyber” and made guest appearances on the FX drama “Pose.”
His acting credits also included appearances in movies including “Varsity Blues” (1998), “The Rules of Attraction” (2002) and the horror movie “Bad Hair” (2020).
Van Der Beek announced he had been diagnosed with colorectal cancer in a statement on Nov. 3, 2024.

“I’ve been privately dealing with this diagnosis and have been taking steps to resolve it, with the support of my incredible family,” he said. “There’s reason for optimism, and I’m feeling good,” he said at the time.
Van Der Beek and wife Kimberly wed in 2010 and share six children: Olivia, Joshua, Annabel, Emilia, Gwendolyn and Jeremiah.
The devoted father often shared images and videos on Instagram showing his life at home on the family’s ranch in Texas.
In September, Van Der Beek was expected to appear alongside his former “Dawson’s Creek” co-stars at a one-night only reunion event in New York City, but he withdrew a day before over health reasons.

The actor explained in an Instagram post that he had been diagnosed with “two stomach viruses” and was too ill to attend the event, a live reading of the series’ pilot episode whose proceeds benefitted the nonprofit F Cancer.
Van Der Beek was replaced onstage by “Hamilton” creator and star Lin-Manuel Miranda.
Van Der Beek ended up appearing in a surprise pre-recorded video at the event to share his gratitude to attendees.
“From the cast to the crew to everybody who’s doing anything and has been so generous, and especially every single last one of you," he said, "you are the best fans in the world."
Van Der Beek sat down with TODAY’s Craig Melvin for an exclusive interview that aired Dec. 19 to talk about life with cancer and how he gained a new perspective after his diagnosis.

“It’s been a longer journey than I ever thought it would be. It’s required more of me — more patience, more discipline, more strength than I knew I had. I knew I was strong. I didn’t know I was this strong. But I feel good,” he told Craig, adding that the biggest change was a new journey of self love.
“What I realized was I’m still worthy of love. My own love, God’s love. And so are we all. That I never would’ve come to realize had I not gone through this journey,” he said.

title: Which Couples Get Engaged on ‘Love Is Blind’ Season 10?
summary: Who gets engaged on Love Is Blind Season 10, set in Columbus, Ohio? All about Emma, Amber, Steven and more couples.
story: Just in time for Valentine’s Day, Netflix’s hit dating show is back for a new season.

“Love Is Blind” Season 10, premiering Feb. 11, introduced fans to so many Ohio-based cast members and couples that viewers might’ve lost track. In the first six episodes, seven couples got engaged in the pods and moved onto the next part of the experiment: a couples trip to Cabo San Lucas, Mexico (or to Malibu, California for one pair).

Like past seasons, plenty of drama unfolded in the pods as multiple love triangles formed and vulnerable moments were shared. One contestant opened up about being a single mom, another spoke about having to undergo multiple surgeries as a child and a third had a relative pass away during filming.

By the end of the first part of the experiment, all the remaining contestants were convinced they had found the person who was meant for them. Get to know the “Love Is Blind” Season 10 couples, and how they fell for each other in the pods, below.
‘Love Is Blind’ Season 10 Couples
Alex H. and Ashley

Alex H. opened up to Ashley about being a former professional soccer player and the injuries he faced throughout his career. He also spoke in his confessional interviews about how looks played a significant role in his past relationships. But, he wanted to sign up for “Love Is Blind” to focus less on physical attraction. Ashley acknowledged in her confessionals that she had “a bad picker” in the past, but she was ready to find her husband. She and Alex H. had undeniable chemistry and she happily accepted his proposal.
Chris and Jessica

Chris spent his time in the pods getting to know Jessica and Bri. He mentioned that one key factor that separated the two were their ages: Bri was 33 and Jessica was 38. However, during his conversations with Jessica, an infectious diseases physician, the two formed a stronger bond. Jessica, who was previously married, spoke about caring for patients during the height of the COVID-19 pandemic and not feeling comforted when she returned home to her husband at night. Chris and Bri mutually decided they weren’t each other’s top choices, and Chris went on to profess his love for Jessica before proposing to her.
Connor and Bri

Connor and Bri both formed multiple connections in the pods. While Bri was busy chatting with Chris, Connor developed an emotional connection with Emma, who considered him her number one option. But in Episode 3, Emma said she could sense that Connor had stronger feelings for another person. He told her she was correct and that he wanted to solely focus on his relationship with Bri. Connor met with Bri in the following episode and she reciprocated his feelings, telling him she was ready to be his “forever stage five clinger.” He responded by thanking her for making him feel secure, and he proposed.
Devo and Brittany W.

Devo and Brittany W.’s relationship wasn’t introduced until Episode 3, but that was likely because the two seemingly only had feelings for each other. Brittany came on the show determined to find her husband. She had already purchased a wedding dress and said she was “manifesting” her dream man walking into the pods. She loved Devo’s “nerdy” voice and he liked that she was ready to have children. He shared with her that he was pulled out of the pods by production and learned that his grandfather had died. He said it was important for him to stay there for the “right reasons” and she comforted him. They got engaged in Episode 4.
Jordan and Amber

Amber shared early in Episode 1 that she is a single mom to a 7-year-old daughter named Emma. Jordan was shown discussing his relationship with the rest of the men, revealing that he was “nervous” about taking on the role of being a dad. He also shared in his confessional interview that when he was interviewing to be on “Love Is Blind” he told producers that having a kid was a “dealbreaker.” Amber told Jordan she was looking for someone to be there for her after a long day. She also said she didn’t feel judged by him. In Episode 2, Jordan read Emma a letter he wrote. It was addressed to “the most beautiful person that I have never seen.” He knelt on one knee and asked her to marry him and she said, “I would love to.”
Mike and Emma

Emma began her time in the pods somewhat guarded despite forming connections with Mike, Connor and Steven. She later opened up with all three about her childhood. Emma was adopted by a white family and never learned the identity of her birth parents. She also was born with birthmarks all over body that had “a very high chance of melanoma,” which meant that she had to have 10 surgeries before the age of 7 and currently has physical scarring. Mike said he would never judge Emma for her appearance. His only concern was that Emma wasn’t sure she wanted to have kids. However, Mike thought that Emma would come around to the idea of being a mom. They exchanged “I love yous” in Episode 5 and got engaged.
Victor and Christine

The first couple introduced in Season 10 was Victor and Christine. Their first conversation was easygoing and both said they were looking to get married. During their second chat, the two discussed their pasts, both revealing they had been charged with misdemeanors that were later expunged. “Match made in heaven,” Victor said after they shared their stories. Christine then recalled challenging moments in her childhood with her parents. When she became emotional, he supported her. “You’re getting emotional because you are a human,” he replied before opening up about one of his “scariest” childhood memories. Their emotional connection continued to grow and the two were already engaged by the end of Episode 1.

title: When Did Madison Chock and Evan Bates Start Dating? Inside the Olympians' Relationship
summary: Who are Madison Chock and Evan Bates? Looking back at the US Olympian ice dancing duo's partnership and romance.
story: Partners on and off the ice, U.S. Olympians Madison Chock and Evan Bates are the dream ice dancing duo.

The three-time and reigning world champion ice dancers first met as teenagers while skating in Michigan, per Team USA.com. They began their official skating partnership in 2011 and started dating five years later.

The pair is competing at the Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics together. They have already won gold this season for the Team USA figure skating team event with famed American skaters including Ilia Malinin, Alysa Liu, and more.
They also revisited their relationship and career story in the new Netflix documentary, "Glitter and Gold."

The 2026 games will be the pair's fourth Olympics as partners.  The one thing missing from their resume is an individual Olympic gold, which they hope to achieve this year. Together, they are three-time world champions and seven-time national champions, per USFigureSkating.org.
"It's the ultimate prize for us in our sport. We've prepared as best as we could. We are so ready," Bates told TODAY in 2026 about the prospect of gold at the 2026 Olympics.
Read on for more about Bates and Choke's relationship ahead of the pair ice dancing event at the Olympics.
They Met as Teenagers in Michigan
At 12, Chock moved to Michigan from her home state of California to pursue skating more seriously.

“I was 12 at that time, and that is a lot of faith to put in a 12-year-old,” Chock told USFigureSkating.org in 2021. “I’m very fortunate to have such incredible parents who have sacrificed their time and their hopes to succeed at my dream.
It was there that she first met Evan Bates, an Ann Arbor, Michigan native and her future partner. In June 2011, when she was 19 years old, Chock began her partnership with Bates.
They Went on 1 Date as Teenagers, But Started Dating Years Later
For Chock's 16th birthday in 2008, the couple went to Bahama Breeze for a date, they remembered to TODAY in 2023.
"Evan took me out to dinner for my 16th birthday to Bahama Breeze," Chock said.
The romance fizzled out, though, as their ice skating partnership grew.
“It was only when we started skating together that we started spending a lot more time together. We started this bond and friendship that eventually grew to a romance,” Bates said.
Then, in 2016, Bates confessed his feelings.

"It took five years before I came forward and expressed my feelings and wanted to be more than skating partners. When that eventually did happen, some of our friends around us, were like, 'Duh,'" he said.
Chock remembers her own POV: "That's interesting. I feel the same way about you," she remembered thinking to TODAY.
They Went Public With Their Relationship Ahead of the 2018 Olympics
After a year of romance, the pair decided to make their relationship public.
In 2018, Bates told TODAY’s Craig Melvin that he “pretty much just told Madi that (he) loved her.”

“Skating together is something that we love doing, and we love doing it together,” Chock told NBC when she confirmed the dating rumors. “Now, we’re together on and off the ice and it makes it even more powerful. It feels completely genuine and real ... it brings us closer together every day.”
Bates Proposed in June 2022
On a trip to Phuket, Thailand, Bates popped the question.

"We were on vacation with friends and they all knew I was going to do it so they stayed out of the way until afterward, then they joined us for champagne and Thai food on the beach," Bates told People. "It was a perfect day, rain included!"
They Got Married in 2024

After 20 years of friendship and 10 years of romance, the pair officially got married in a storybook ceremony in 2024. The wedding was located in Hawaii, where Chock's parents met. Chock's father is of Chinese-Hawaiian descent, per NBC.
Chock posted several times to Instagram to celebrate the occasion. In one post, she shared a video of the newlyweds dancing at sunset.
"Sunset dance as husband and wife 🌅" she captioned the post.
How Their Romance Impacts Their Skating
The couple says that their romantic relationship has only helped grow their on-ice partnership.

“Knowing that we have made the ultimate commitment to be partners for the rest of our lives, we understand how grateful and lucky we are to do what we love with the person we love," Bates told TODAY in 2026.

title: Jill Zarin Fired From 'RHONY' Reunion After Bad Bunny Super Bowl Comments: What to Know
summary: Jill Zarin, an original cast member of “The Real Housewives of New York City,” has been fired from the upcoming reunion series, “The Golden Life,” TODAY.com has confirmed
story: Jill Zarin, an original cast member of “The Real Housewives of New York City,” has been fired from the upcoming reunion series, “The Golden Life,” TODAY.com has confirmed.

The E! show has yet to go into production, according to Variety, who was first to report the news, but the reality star came under fire for comments she made about Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show.
Why Was Jill Zarin Fired From the 'RHONY' Reunion?
Zarin was a cast member of “RHONY” from 2008 to 2011. A 10-episode reunion, titled  “The Golden Life,” was announced earlier this month, per Variety.
It is set to also include Luann de Lesseps, Ramona Singer, Kelly Bensimon and Sonja Morgan as they “reunite with their iconic New York City friend group in glamorous Palm Beach.”
However, Zarin will no longer be a part of the show after she posted a since-deleted Instagram video criticizing Bad Bunny’s performance on Super Bowl Sunday, Feb. 8.

Blink49 Studios, the producers for “The Golden Life,” confirmed Zarin’s firing in a statement to TODAY.com on Feb. 10. While they mention “recent public comments” made by Zarin, they did not specifically note that it was regarding the statements she made about Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl show.

“In light of recent public comments made by Jill Zarin, Blink49 Studios has decided not to move forward with her involvement in ‘The Golden Life,’” the statement reads. “We remain committed to delivering the series in line with our company standards and values.”
Reps for Zarin did not immediately respond to TODAY.com's request for comment.
What Did Jill Zarin Say About Bad Bunny's Super Bowl Halftime Show?
In her Instagram video, which she has now deleted, Zarin criticized Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl halftime show for being in Spanish and lacking white people, NBC News reports.
Zarin called it “the worst halftime show ever” and noted that she doesn’t speak Spanish.
“I would’ve liked to have known the words that he was saying,” Zarin said. “To me it looked like a political statement because there were literally no white people in the entire thing.”
Lady Gaga, who is white, made a surprise appearance during the set and sang a salsa version of her duet with Bruno Mars, “Die With a Smile.” TikToker Alix Earle was also spotted in La Casita.

The former reality star believed that show “was an ICE thing,” referencing U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement and the current turmoil around mass deportations happening throughout the country. She also said the NFL sold out.
Zarin received a slew of backlash and criticism, with people reposting her videos and calling her out for her comments. She has since disabled the comments on her Instagram.

title: New Vogue Editor Chloe Malle Speaks On 'Nepo Baby' Criticism
summary: Vogue editor in chief Chloe Malle speaks to Jenna Bush for her first TV interview about nepo baby criticism, relationship with Anna Wintour and more.
story: Chloe Malle has some big heels to fill as she steps into her new role as head of editorial content of American Vogue, but she's up for the challenge.
Last June, Anna Wintour announced that she was stepping down from the position after nearly 40 years of running the fashion publication. Three months later, Malle was named the new head of editorial.

“I really never sincerely thought that would be me, and I think that it still isn’t because Anna, I do feel, is very present and I appreciate that," Malle told TODAY's Jenna Bush Hager in a segment that aired on Feb. 10.
Wintour is chief content officer for Condé Nast and global editorial director at Vogue.
Though Wintour stepped away from her editor-in-chief role, she previously revealed that she would be staying on at Condé Nast as chief content officer and global editorial director at Vogue.
While chatting with Malle, Jenna noted that Wintour shared the following message about her successor: "She said, 'I’m excited to continue working with her as her mentor, but also as her student.'"
Malle was touched by the kind words and responded, saying, "I’m not sure what exactly I’m teaching Anna, but I hope that it can be exciting and unexpected."

Malle may be new to the editor-in-chief role, but the 40-year-old has spent more than a decade overseeing the magazine's digital content and podcast, "The Run-Through." She became editor of Vogue.com in 2023, per the publication. In total, she's been with Vogue for 15 years.

The Vogue veteran grew up in a famous family: Her mother is actor Candice Bergen and her father was filmmaker Louis Malle, who passed away when Chloe was a child. Because of this, many people have called Malle a "nepo baby" throughout her career. Still, it's a label that doesn't phase her.
Malle refers to herself as a "proud nepo baby" — not because she has advantages that others don't, but because she's "fiercely proud" of her parents.
"My mother, she was working more than five days a week filming 'Murphy Brown' and working 'til 2 a.m. on Fridays," she said.
Even with her hectic schedule, Bergen always made her daughter feel "like the only thing that mattered."
"She was really smart. She really built a community around me that made me feel loved and supported at all times," Malle said.
Malle said, in her own career, that having a famous family was actually impetus for her to work harder.
"I’ve always wanted to prove to people that I am more than that, and that I can work harder than anyone to show you that I’m here for the long haul," she said.

"Someone recently in a media report sort of dismissively called me a workhorse. And I was like, That’s the nicest thing you can say about me. I’d much rather be called a workhorse than a show pony," she continued.
As a mom of two, Malle wants to replicate the feeling her mom gave her for her own children. She said her husband, Graham Albert, has stepped up as she stepped into her new role.

"I am trying to prioritize things that I really care about doing with my kids, but my husband takes them to the dentist at 3 p.m. on a Wednesday, and I just have accepted that that’s not going to be my role, and that’s OK," she said.
This month, Malle will celebrate an important milestone: her first physical issue as head of editorial content. Rosalía covers the issue, which features Malle's first editor's letter.

"In my first editor's letter, I talked about how for Halloween two years ago, I dressed up as Ms. Frizzle from the 'Magic School Bus.' You know, Ms. Frizzle always dresses to reflect the things she's excited about and the places she wants to go and see, and it is about magic," she said.
"So what is the type of story that I passionately am excited about? And that can be a little kooky. That can be something that maybe feels off the beaten path."
Listen to the full interview between Jenna and Chloe Malle here!

title: Backstreet’s Back to Launch Pizza Hut’s Shape Of My Heart-Shaped Pizza
summary: Pizza Hut is teaming up with the Backstreet Boys (Nick Carter and Howie Dorough) this February for a Shape of My Heart-Shaped Pizza in honor of Valentine's Day.
story: Show me the meaning ... of being hungry.
As Valentine’s Day inches closer, Pizza Hut is partnering with two boy-band heartthrobs to pander to millennials and help make Feb. 14 a bit more romantic this year.

The restaurant chain has teamed up with Backstreet Boys Nick Carter and Howie Dorough to release a heart-shaped pizza with a fun name that’s a play on one of the band’s biggest hits: “Shape of My Heart.”
Eager to get your hands on the Shape of My Heart-Shaped Pizza? Here are all the details.
The Shape of My Heart-Shaped Pizza Is Available Through Feb. 22
Between Feb. 10 — 22, Pizza Hut customers can order a medium one-topping Shape of My Heart-Shaped Pizza in-store, online or in the chain’s app. Prices start at $11.99.
Nick Carter and Howie Dorough Star in a New Campaign for the Pizza

Carter and Dorough, two of the five members of the Backstreet Boys, know a thing or two about setting hearts aflutter and they’re lending their romantic skills to a new video campaign for the Shape of My Heart-Shaped Pizza.
The bandmates cheese it up and pose with the heart-shaped pizzas while striking fun poses, competing in a “This or That” heartthrob edition and debate hand-heart gestures.
Pizza Hut Recently Announced the Closure of Hundreds of Restaurants

Yum! Brands, the parent company of Pizza Hut, recently announced plans to close hundreds of Pizza Hut restaurants across the United States in 2026. The news came during the company’s Q4 2025 earnings call on Feb. 4.
Approximately 250 Pizza Hut locations in the U.S. will be closed in the first half of 2026. The company has not shared a list of the specific restaurants.
Yum! Brands chief financial officer Ranjith Roy mentioned “targeted closures of underperforming units” while outlining the company’s financial performance over the last year.

title: Boy Who Went Viral in Bad Bunny's Halftime Show Speaks About the Experience
summary: Who did Bad Bunny give a Grammy to during his halftime performance? Lincoln Fox is a child actor and his mother gave an interview to TODAY.com about the moment.
story: One day, Lincoln Fox will look back and realize how cool it was to play a young Bad Bunny in the Puerto Rican superstar's Super Bowl halftime show.

But for now, the child actor is more interested in the things that excite most 5-year-olds, like getting to miss school, playing with toy cars and practicing the popular "67" slang trend — all the things he enjoyed most about rehearsing for the performance.
Millions of viewers tuned into the Super Bowl halftime show on Feb. 8 to watch Bad Bunny sing some of his greatest hits.
During the performance, Lincoln sat alongside two actors playing Bad Bunny’s parents and watched a small TV that was airing the musician’s recent Grammys acceptance speech.
When Bad Bunny came over to the group, he gave Lincoln the award and patted his head in a symbolic gesture to show that dreams do come true.
When asked how he felt when he first learned that he’d be appearing in the halftime show, Lincoln gets right to the point and tells TODAY.com, “Good.”
However, he was a little bit confused about the musician's name at first.
“He said, ‘Why is the Bad Bunny evil?’ He wasn’t too sure how that all worked,” his mom, Erika Ramadan, replies.
“Because he has a ‘bad’ in the first name. That means that he’s bad,” Lincoln explains.
During rehearsals, Lincoln got to interact with Bad Bunny, whose real name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio, on several occasions.
“He was really sweet with Lincoln. I don’t know what he said to him, but he spoke to him when he was doing the rehearsals and would pat his head,” Ramadan says.
When Lincoln’s agent asked Ramadan to submit a funny video of Lincoln for a potential gig, the family never expected it would be for the Super Bowl.
“Apparently he bears a very strong resemblance to a young Bad Bunny,” Lincoln's mom says.

Some social media users also thought Lincoln bore a resemblance to Liam Conejo Ramos, a 5-year-old who was detained by ICE agents in Minneapolis, along with his father. Conejo, coincidentally, means bunny in Spanish.
Ramadan heard the rumors and says she can understand why people mistook her son for Ramos.
“I’m not upset at all about the confusion. If anything, I feel like it brought back a very important message: that there are children being detained and separated from their families," she says.

Ramadan included a mention of Ramos in the post-Super Bowl post she shared to her son's Instagram page, writing, "Sending love to Liam Ramos. We all deserve peace and love in America, a country built by and home to so many hard-working immigrants."

While reflecting on her son's appearance in the Super Bowl halftime show, Ramadan notes that the moment was particularly special since Lincoln, who didn’t start talking until he was 3-and-a-half years old, first got into acting to help build his social skills.
She marvels at "how much confidence he’s built in the last year and a half."
"How much more he’s talking and how he interacts with adults and how he follows directions, all these really important life lessons and skills that he’s building."

title: The Special Meaning Behind Bad Bunny's Super Bowl Halftime Show
summary: Bad Bunny celebrated the history, culture and pride of Puerto Rico with his historic Super Bowl 2026 halftime show.
story: Bad Bunny celebrated the history, culture and pride of Puerto Rico with his historic Super Bowl 2026 halftime show.

The Puerto Rican rapper and singer, who just made history at the 2026 Grammys when his all-Spanish album “DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS” took home album of the year, continued his historic streak by being the first male Latino artist tapped to lead the biggest music performance of the year.
He didn’t take the opportunity for granted. From the moment his halftime show started on Sunday, Feb. 8, it was clear Bad Bunny was going to make a statement.

The first image shown was a farm worker wearing a pava while in a real-life sugar cane field, who kicked off the show by saying in Spanish, “Qué rico es ser latino,” which translates to, “How wonderful it is to be Latino.”
Bad Bunny then emerged from a re-creation of that sugar cane field on the Super Bowl stage also surrounded by workers wearing the wide-brimmed straw hat from Puerto Rico.
During the show, he rapped and sang hits like “Tití Me Preguntó,” “Baile Inolvidable,” “DtMF” and more songs.
Then, he watched a real couple tie the knot and ended his 13-minute set by naming every country in North and South America and holding a football that read “Together We Are America.”
The unforgettable performance also featured surprise appearances by Lady Gaga and Ricky Martin and countless Easter eggs.
Read on for a breakdown of the meaning behind all the special moments in Bad Bunny’s Super Bowl halftime performance.
Sugar Cane Fields and Pavas

Bad Bunny opened his performance with a wide shot panning to show workers wearing pavas, traditional Puerto Rican straw hats, and chopping sugar cane in fields while text on the screen read “Benito Antonio Martinez Ocasio presenta el espectáculo de medio tiempo del Súper Tazón.”

Bad Bunny appeared in between the workers, who represented the history of sugar cane plantations and the brutal labor past generations endured. The scene, per a press release, paid “homage to Latino labor, heritage and the generations who laid the groundwork before him.”
Ocasio and 64

The rapper and singer sported an all-white ensemble that included his version of a football jersey with the name Ocasio and number 64 on the back. The 31-year-old’s outfit honored his mother, Lysaurie Ocasio, with the last name.
In a statement shared with Rolling Stone, Bad Bunny clarified that 1964 was the year his late uncle Cutito was born.

“The little I know about the NFL is thanks to him. He left for the United States at 17 to work and never returned to live in Puerto Rico, although he always visited us in late January or early February and stayed at our house. That was right in the middle of the NFL postseason, so I always watched the games with him,” he shared in a statement translated from Spanish. “He was a huge fan of the San Francisco 49ers, the team whose home stadium is where this Super Bowl 60 took place, the one in which I performed.”
He said Cutito died two years ago, around the time when the 49ers lost to the Kansas City Chiefs in the 2024 Super Bowl.

“I always dreamed of taking my uncle to a Super Bowl and I couldn’t. He left unexpectedly, without warning,” he continued. “So during my Super Bowl halftime show, I decided to have him on my shirt: OCASIO, his last name, the same as my mother’s, and his birth year, 64. I dedicated my performance to him before it began. I’m sure he saw it, he was present, and he felt proud of his nephew. Now all that’s left is the day the 49ers win another Super Bowl.”
Nods to Puerto Rican and Latino Social Culture

As he began to rap the words to “Tití Me Preguntó,” he made his way through a re-creation of Puerto Rico’s countryside, per a press release. He passed many small businesses, like a nail salon and piragua stand (Puerto Rican snow cones) and a group of men playing dominoes.
The taco stand featured, Villa’s Tacos, is a real Los Angeles-based business.
He also included real-life Puerto Rican boxer Xander Zayas and Mexican American boxer Emiliano Vargas, training in between the vendors.
La Casita from His Residency Travels to Santa Clara
One of the main set pieces from Bad Bunny’s sold-out residency last year, La Casita, made its way to the Super Bowl stage, and continued the tradition of including a few celebrity appearances.
Some of the stars spotted dancing on the La Casita set were Cardi B, Jessica Alba, Pedro Pascal, Karol G, Young Miko, Ronald Acuña Jr. and Dave Grutman.
La Casita is a representation of a typical Puerto Rican home, with Bad Bunny specifically touching on the tradition of party de marquesina, a party house where everyone gathers.
Honoring Puerto Rican Reggaeton Legends
During one portion of her performance, Bad Bunny gave a nod to the reggaeton legends that influenced him.
As he exits La Casita, a remix of Daddy Yankee's “Gasolina,” Tego Calderón's “Pa’ Que Retozen” and Don Omar “Dale Don Dale,” played.
In Spanish, Bad Bunny told the crowd, “You’re listening to music from Puerto Rico. From the barrios and the projects,” before singing “EoO.”
A Couple Says ‘I Do’
The wedding that took place during Bad Bunny’s set was real. He extended an invitation to the couple to participate in his show after they previously invited him to their nuptials.
He also signed their marriage certificate and served as their witness, according to a press release.
Lady Gaga Performs Salsa Version of ‘Die With a Smile’
Lady Gaga gave a new rendition of her hit duet “Die With a Smile” with Bruno Mars for the halftime show, adding a salsa flair thanks to an arrangement by Big Jay.
After she finished, Bad Bunny took her by the hand and the two performed a short salsa routine.

Bad Bunny has repeatedly spoken in the past about how much he admires Lady Gaga and considers himself a Little Monster, the name of her fanbase. He mentioned being inspired by her in a 2020 Rolling Stone article. When speaking to E! News that same year, he revealed he listens to the singer’s “Chromatica” album when he is feeling down.

“In that moment where I feel sad, it’s ‘Chromatica’ (by) Lady Gaga,” he said at the time. “That album makes me feel more happy. When she dropped that album, I was in a really good moment, enjoying my life. I love that album.”
Puerto Rican Social Club Owner Toñita

Bad Bunny made his way back to the center of the field where a marqueta and barber shop set was placed. In the middle of performing “NUEVAYoL,” he was handed a shot by Toñita, the owner of the last Latino social club in Williamsburg, New York.
A Grammy Moment and Dreaming Big
Footage was shown on a small television of Bad Bunny accepting his album of the year Grammy the weekend prior. The television was being watched by a mother, father and their young son.
Bad Bunny walked onto the set and handed a Grammy to the adorable child, Lincoln Fox.
The artist’s team described the moment as “a powerful reminder of possibility, that we are all that child at some point, dreaming big and believing in what could be.”

Adding, “The moment resonated as a message to every young person watching, that with belief and perseverance, they too can one day stand where he stands now, from the Grammys stage to the Super Bowl.”
Puerto Rican Legend Ricky Martin Performs

For his second special guest, Bad Bunny brought out Puerto Rico’s own Ricky Martin. The icon delivered a moving performance of “Lo Que Paso a Hawaii,” Bad Bunny’s song about not wanting Puerto Rico to be colonized or gentrified like Hawaii.
Martin performed the track in a set that re-created Bad Bunny’s “DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS” album cover.

Martin’s involvement comes a week after he penned an op-ed celebrating Bad Bunny after his major Grammy win, writing in the Puerto Rican newspaper El Nuevo Día: “I know what it means to succeed without letting go of where you come from. That’s why what you have achieved is not just a historic musical accomplishment, it’s a cultural and human victory.⁠”
Power Lines and the Puerto Rican Flag
The next elaborate set on the field was a series of power lines. Bad Bunny held the Puerto Rican flag before climbing up one of the power lines.

In 2024, Puerto Rico experienced an extreme power outage that left nearly 80% of the island in total darkness. The island has repeatedly experienced power outages since being hit by the Category 4 Hurricane Maria in 2017.
‘God Bless America’

As his set came to a close, Bad Bunny highlighted every country in the Americas. Flag bearers holding flags for each country began sprinting across the field as Bad Bunny said, “God Bless America!” He named every country while the message “The only thing more powerful than hate is love” was displayed behind him.
He spiked a football that read, “Together We Are America” and ended with a roaring performance of “DtMF,” a love letter to Puerto Rico.

title: Beloved L.A. Taco Shop Featured in Bad Bunny’s Halftime Show: ‘This One Is for All the Immigrants’
summary: Victor Villa, Mexican American owner of beloved Los Angeles taco shop Villa's Tacos, appeared in Bad Bunny's Super Bowl halftime show with a taco cart.
story: A Los Angeles taco shop owner got the chance of a lifetime to appear with one of the world’s biggest artists on one of the world’s biggest stages.

On Feb. 8, Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio — otherwise known as  Bad Bunny — headlined a historic Super Bowl halftime show, performing a medley of hits, entirely in Spanish, to a cheering stadium and a TV audience of millions.
Bad Bunny included iconic Latin American places and people in his performance, like the owner of Villa’s Tacos, Victor Villa, a first-generation Mexican American who was born and raised in L.A.

Villa’s appearance was during the first song, “Tití Me Preguntó.” After Bad Bunny emerges from Puerto Rican sugarcane fields, passing multiple roadside stops, he takes a piragua (a Puerto Rican shaved ice dessert) from a vendor, then turns to Villa, who is manning a taco cart, and hands it to him.
The taquero graciously accepts the icy treat, which many say symbolizes a bridge between two popular Latin American food traditions.

“I started my business in 2018 at my grandma’s house with nothing more than just a grill, you know, a canopy, tables, the first round of goods and nothing more than dreams, aspirations and hopes,” Villa tells TODAY.com from Santa Clara, California.
He’s still in town post-performance and reflecting on his station. “Through blood, sweat and tears, we were able to open a restaurant five years later,” he says.

Villa’s Tacos grew from one location into three, boasting multiple wins from L.A. Taco’s Taco Madness championship and a Michelin Bib Gourmand award three years in a row for offerings like his queso taco with onion, cilantro, guacamole, cotija cheese and crema Mexicana.

“I couldn’t have sold that 1st taco, if my parents didn’t make the difficult decision to leave their homeland for a better life & immigrate to the U.S.,” Villa wrote in an Instagram post about his Super Bowl appearance. “This one is for all the immigrants who paved the way before us to make this moment possible. Villa’s Tacos is a product of immigrants.”
Villa says he’s honored to have been chosen to take part in this monumental celebration of Latin American culture.

“It was a true testament to Bunny’s character and who he is to want to bring the country together and make immigrants part of something big in a time where the country feels very divided,” he says. “Immigrants bring so much to the beauty of this country through the love that they share, through the food that they make, through the smiles that they give.”

Villa says he had to keep his participation secret for weeks during rehearsals, but he bonded with several of the other people who made cameos, like Toñita, the 85-year-old owner of the Caribbean Social Club in Brooklyn, New York.

“Like, to you guys watching, you might look at them as like, ‘Oh, that’s just the piragua vendor,’ but it’s not just the piragua vendor, that’s Juan Pablo, and he’s the kindest soul ever. He loves his family. He also plays professional basketball,” Villa says. “And then there’s the coconut vendors: You got Swat, who’s a super talented B-boy, and he has a heart of gold. He loves his kids so much.”
During the performance, the jumbotron read, “The only thing more powerful than hate is love,” a poignant message that resonates with Villa.

“My grandma, who came from Mexico, is the one who showed me the secrets to great cooking. But it’s not only a secret that applies to great cooking, but applies to living a happy life,” he says. “She’s the best chef that I know personally, and I would always ask her, ‘Wow. Why is your food so good?’ and she said ‘Mm, because I put a lot of love in there.’”

title: What Were the Best Super Bowl Ads? These 3 Commercials Came Out on Top
summary: USA Today's Ad Meter ranked the best commercials from Super Bowl 60. USA Today entertainment reporter Ralphie Aversa breaks down the top three.
story: The Super Bowl: Come for the game, stay for the ads.

Yes, Super Bowl 60 had more than its fair share of memorable commercials that helped fill the time between plays, as the Seattle Seahawks toppled the New England Patriots, 29-14, to win the franchise’s second championship.
USA Today has released its list of the best commercials from the big game in its 38th annual Ad Meter.
The average price for a 30-second spot was $8 million, so brands definitely wanted to stretch their dollars as much as they could.
USA Today entertainment reporter Ralphie Aversa said this year once again featured a lot of celebrities, but also noted there was another prevalent theme.
“We saw a lot of familiar songs, heard those songs really driving narratives throughout these commercials,” he said Feb. 9 on the 3rd hour of TODAY.
Here’s a look at the top three commercials, according to the USA Today Ad Meter.
First place

“American Icons” was set to Lynyrd Skynyrd’s “Free Bird” and homed in on the relationship between a bird and one of Budweiser’s famous Clydesdales, with symbolism celebrating the brand’s 150th anniversary and the United States’ 250th birthday.
“Budweiser now winning the USA Today Ad Meter for its record 10th time. That’s a stretch, those 10 wins, dating back to 1999,” Aversa said.
Second place

“Last Harvest” was an emotional spot set to a version of Keane’s “Somewhere Only We Know” that featured a potato farmer for Lay’s handing the keys to his farm to his daughter. Directed by Academy Award winner Taika Waititi, Aversa noted the ad is actually based on a real family and spoke to another recurring theme in the game’s commercials.
“We saw brands really trying to tug at our heartstrings. We saw it with Lay’s,” he said.
Third place

“The Choice” was set to Queen’s “I Want to Break Free” and had plenty of humor. Coke has a polar bear as a mascot, so this ad featured one of the animals stunned to discover it chose Pepsi Zero Sugar over Coke Zero in a taste test and then trying to come to terms with it. This commercial was also directed by Waititi, who made a cameo in the spot, which also featured a nod to the Coldplay kiss cam scandal from last summer.

“Obviously the USA Today Ad Meter panelists, they were in on the joke, first with the polar bears, who are obviously mascots for another drink brand, and the fact of the Pepsi Challenge, which is marking its 50-year anniversary,” Aversa said.
“But then also at the very end of that ad, we see a little nod to the whole Coldplay concert cam Astronomer scandal, and yes, even America was in on that joke as well.”

Dunkin’’s “Good Will Dunkin,” which featured a cavalcade of sitcom stars from the ‘80s and ‘90s took fourth place, while Michelob ULTRA’s “The ULTRA Instructor,” with Kurt Russell, Lewis Pullman, Chloe Kim and T.J. Oshie placed fifth.

title: The Wedding During Bad Bunny's Halftime Show Was Real. The Dress Designer Shares How It Happened
summary: Wedding dress designer Hayley Paige shares how the Bad Bunny halftime show wedding came to be.
story: One couple invited Bad Bunny to their wedding and got the surprise of a lifetime when the musician asked them to take part in his Super Bowl 60 halftime show instead.
The couple said "I do" during the epic performance on Sunday, Feb. 8. Bad Bunny even signed the marriage certificate, per a press release sent to TODAY.com.
For the occasion, the bride wore a white wedding gown from designer Hayley Paige.
Paige tells TODAY.com she first learned about the exciting moment from a friend of her sister, Megan Gutman, a producer who has worked on several Super Bowl commercials before.

“Her really good friend Joleen Garnett is a stylist, and she had texted me maybe two weeks ago and said, ‘I need a dress for a really important event.’ She just basically said, ‘Could you send me a bunch of options? Please don’t share it with anyone, but it is for the Super Bowl halftime show,’" Paige explains.

The designer was psyched that one of her dresses could be featured in the halftime show, but she didn't want to get her hopes up since she assumed that Garnett was also pulling dresses from other designers as well.
Paige originally thought the dress might be for a backup dancer, but then she learned that it was for an actual bride.
“To have a moment like that, and it actually be a real bride, I think made it even more special because the message is, ‘Love wins,’” she says.
The dress, named Becoming Jane, is part of Paige's new Twice Upon a Time collection and can be worn in six different ways.

“This one has some really fun interchangeability. She has a fitted base, which is lace on lace, kind of this textured, sexy fit. It’s got a slit. Then we build up on the dress so there’s a big overskirt that you can wear, so you can kind of have your cake and eat it too — except it’s have your fitted look and then also a ball gown," she says.
Brides can also customize the look with half sleeves or a detachable bolero.
"It’s a fun shapeshifter of a dress. (The bride) wore it just with the base and the slit and then had a beautiful, matching veil with it to show off her curves and she looks fabulous," Paige says.
Paige says the dress has a lot of “sexiness” and “feminine energy” to it. Plus, it has a stretchy lining to make it easy to dance in, which was especially helpful in this instance.
“I saw during the show they were dancing and having fun,” she says.
While watching the halftime show at home with her fiancé, friend and dogs, Paige felt like it was a full circle moment for her brand and her own story.

“I’ve been through this crazy legal battle where I lost my name and then got it all back in a bankruptcy court. I've been on this journey. I actually rebranded and relaunched Hayley Paige just six months ago," she says.
After the halftime show, Paige contacted the bride to share her well wishes.
"I reached out to the bride to congratulate her and tell her she looks beautiful and that she can keep the dress," she says.

title: Every Song in Bad Bunny's Super Bowl Halftime Show Setlist
summary: What songs did Bad Bunny perform at the 2026 Super Bowl halftime show? Here's a list of the songs Bad Bunny sang at Super Bowl 60.
story: The Bad Bunny Bowl is here!
After being unable to sing at the Grammys despite host Trevor Noah's plea, the Puerto Rican superstar finally let loose on Super Bowl 60.
The dance-filled performance also featured appearances by Lady Gaga, who sang her hit with Bruno Mars, "Die with a Smile," and Ricky Martin.
Cardi B, Karol G, Pedro Pascal, Jessica Alba and Alix Earle were all spotted in Bad Bunny's Casita during the performance.
Bad Bunny performed amid a set of tall grass that suddenly appeared at Levi's Stadium, plus the accompaniment of a live brass band.

Bad Bunny — whose given name is Benito Antonio Martínez Ocasio — teased his historic halftime show during a conversation with Zane Lowe and Ebro Darden for Apple Music on Feb. 5, saying it would be a “huge party.”
“It’s going to be what people can always expect from me,” he continued, joking that understanding Spanish isn't a requirement for enjoying the show.
“I know that I told them that they had four months to learn Spanish. They don’t even have to learn Spanish. It’s better if they learn to dance,” he said.
That said, if people wanted to follow along, lyrics to the songs appeared on Jumbotrons within the stadium.
The performance celebrated Puerto Rican and Latin American culture, all while taking the audience through Bad Bunny’s career, all the way through his latest album, “Debí Tirar Más Fotos.”

The global star was hand-selected by Jay-Z for the milestone Super Bowl 60. It comes just one week after he won the marquee award of the Grammys, album of the year, and spoke out against the Trump administration during his acceptance speech.
“Before I say thanks to God, I’m going to say: ‘ICE out.’ We’re not savage, we’re not animals, we’re not aliens,” he continued. “We are humans, and we are Americans."

title: Ben Affleck Unites ’90s Sitcom Stars in Dunkin’s Chaotic and Nostalgic Super Bowl Ad
summary: Dunkin's nostalgic Super Bowl ad (“Good Will Dunkin’”) stars Ben Affleck, Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc, Jason Alexander and several other '90s sitcom greats.
story: The stars — specifically, sitcom stars — have aligned at Dunkin’ for its latest Super Bowl spectacle.

Dunkin’ just dropped its 2026 Super Bowl ad, after airing a teaser of Ben Affleck pitching a “cringe” VHS tape to fellow ’90s sitcom stars Jennifer Aniston, Matt LeBlanc and Jason Alexander the week prior.

In the minute-long commercial, Affleck reveals the contents of the tape he showed the trio in the teaser: a mashup of at least six sitcoms, several stars and one movie, in what he calls “Good Will Dunkin.’”
While last year’s batty, coffee bean-covered, DunKings-themed ad also starred Affleck, with cameos from Tom Brady, Matt Damon and Jeremy Strong, this year’s pivots from music to television.

It’s a never-aired sitcom pilot set in 1995 — the year the brand put iced coffee on its menu. The episode follows the accidental “invention” of iced coffee inside a Dunkin’ in Cambridge, Massachusetts, by a cast of familiar faces — nine of them, to be exact.
“Before the movie, a much better version of ‘Good Will Hunting’ was made as a sitcom with a real genius in the lead and some other actors,” Affleck says at the start of the commercial.
The ad imagines a small-screen version of the Oscar-winning film with Affleck — instead of Damon — as Will. And instead of doing janitorial work, Will is employed at a Dunkin’ by boss Alexander.
“Hey, Will, did you arrange the Munchkins in the Fibonacci sequence?” Alexander, known for his role as George Costanza on “Seinfeld,” asks. “I got a genius working for me.”
Nostalgic, cringeworthy sitcom tropes dominate the commerical, as actors recite iconic lines from “Friends” and other shows of the time.

Referencing a Costanza line, Jasmine Guy, who played Whitney Gilbert on “A Different World,” says, “I will marry the first man that can help me with the Fibonacci sequence,” and several men gather to help.
“‘A Different World’ will always be family to me, so stepping back into that era for Dunkin’ felt like coming home,” Guy tells TODAY.com. “Sharing the set with this cast made it a real highlight.”
Aniston appears at the end of the ad, after breaking up with Affleck’s character, to show off her new boyfriend.
“You like doughnuts?” Aniston asks.
“Yeah,” Affleck replies.
“Well, this is my new boyfriend,” Aniston says, pulling Brady into frame. She asks, “How do you like ‘deez nuts’?” in a reference to Dr. Dre’s 1992 song.
The cast shrugs, ending the ad but not the memory of the former Patriot’s salad-bowl-shaped wig, worthy of its own laugh track.
The corny commercial is full of gasp-worthy cameos from sitcom greats — including one 78-year-old who appears just as he did in the ’80s.
“Dunkin’ brought the right people together with such a funny idea, and the energy on set made it all click,” Ted Danson tells TODAY.com. “It was a really fun day.”
There is so much star power in this commercial, we thought a bulleted list of actors in order of appearance may be helpful:

Ben Affleck — never a sitcom star, but did appear once in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.”Jason Alexander — George Costanza from “Seinfeld” Matt LeBlanc — Joey Tribbiani from “Friends”Jasmine Guy — Whitney Gilbert from “A Different World”Alfonso Ribeiro — Carlton Banks from “The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air”Jaleel White — Steve Urkel/Stefan Urquelle from “Family Matters”Ted Danson — Sam Malone from “Cheers”Jennifer Aniston — Rachel Green from “Friends”Tom Brady — never a sitcom star, but a football great in a wacky wig
Ben Affleck — never a sitcom star, but did appear once in “Buffy the Vampire Slayer.”
Jason Alexander — George Costanza from “Seinfeld”
Matt LeBlanc — Joey Tribbiani from “Friends”
Jasmine Guy — Whitney Gilbert from “A Different World”
Alfonso Ribeiro — Carlton Banks from “The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air”
Jaleel White — Steve Urkel/Stefan Urquelle from “Family Matters”
Ted Danson — Sam Malone from “Cheers”
Jennifer Aniston — Rachel Green from “Friends”
Tom Brady — never a sitcom star, but a football great in a wacky wig

title: Who Is Ann Michael Maye? All About Drake Maye's Wife
summary: Who is Drake Maye's wife, Ann Michael Maye? Here's what to know about Ann Michael Maye including her career as a food TikToker, her NBC show, her athletic career, her age and more.
story: From high school football games to Super Bowl LX, Patriots quarterback Drake Maye has had the same girl cheering on the sidelines for him.
The QB met his wife, Ann Michael Maye, formerly Ann Michael Hudson, all the way back in middle school.

Going into nearly two years of marriage, Drake Maye, 23, has an opportunity to claim another ring this year with hopes of winning this year’s Super Bowl, where the Patriots are set to face off against the Seattle Seahawks.

“Her watching my middle school football games playing quarterback to now getting a chance to watch me in the super bowl … I wouldn’t rather do it with anybody else,” Drake Maye said in a press conference.
At the big match, Ann Michael Maye, 22, will be cheering Drake Maye on.

“WE ARE GOING TO THE SUPER BOWL!!!!!” Ann Michael Maye wrote in the caption of an Instagram post shared after the Patriots’ AFC Championship win. “Words can’t even begin to describe how proud I am of you, #10!!!! Watching your dreams come true is the biggest blessing and I love you!!!!!”
Here’s what to know about Ann Michael Maye and her own formidable career.
What Ann Michael Maye Is Wearing to the Super Bowl
Ahead of the game, Ann Michael Maye posted a snippet of her Super Bowl weekend on Instagram, with a carousel that included fit checks, dinner out and a game of mahjong with her husband.
Then, photos of her with Drake Maye on game day show her wearing a vintage Patriots jacket and polka dot pants.
She shared a mirror selfie of her outfit on her Instagram story.
The Couple Shared a Sweet Kiss Before the Game Began
The couple was photographed sharing a kiss before the Super Bowl began.
She wrote on her Instagram story, “Proud is an understatement. I love you @drakemaye,” next to a photo of them kissing.
They Met in Middle School
The couple have been linked since middle school, according to a TikTok throwback Ann Michael Maye posted in 2024.
The couple celebrated their nine-year anniversary in 2024.
“9 years with you and it keeps getting better everyday❤️ I couldn’t imagine my life with out you as my best friend. I love you more than you know!” Ann Michael Maye wrote on Instagram.
Both Attended the University of North Carolina
After high school, Drake Maye went on to play football at University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill starting in 2021.
Ann Michael Maye also went to UNC, studying business administration with an entrepreneurship and conflict management double minor, according to her LinkedIn.
She was also the vice president of finance at her sorority, Kappa Delta. She also completed two strategy internships.
The Couple Married in 2025
Drake Maye got down on one knee with an ocean view and announced their engagement on Instagram on January 15, 2025.
“Love doing life with you. Can’t wait to marry you!” he wrote in a caption.
The couple exchanged vows later that year on June 21, according to Ann Michael Maye’s Instagram. The next day, they shared photos from the wedding on Instagram.
“What a night! Cheers to forever🥂I love you!” Drake Maye wrote.
Drake Maye Is Happy He Married Young
During a conference with the media ahead of the Super Bowl, Drake Maye shared the relationship advice he gives his Patriots teammates.

“My teammates have the right to whatever they choose but I would definitely advise them to eventually get married,” he said. “It’s one of the best things I’ve experienced in my life. There’s no better feeling than coming home to a wife that loves you and cares for you.”
“I know my teammates, some of them are experiencing different parts of their life. I chose to get married young, and I don’t regret it one bit,” he continued.
She’s an Athlete
Ann Michael Maye played sports in high school, including softball and basketball.
“She’s a great athlete,” Drake Maye said at a press conference. “She beats me at a lot of sports.”
Ann Michael Maye Is Now a Food Influencer
The 22-year-old has a large social media presence. She shares cooking and day-in-the-life content with over 500,000 followers on TikTok.
Leading up to Christmas of last year, she baked something different for 25 days as part of a challenge called Bakemas.

In early 2026, she debuted her NBC Sports Boston baking show, “Beyond Bakemas.” So far, she’s been joined by guests like Bartsool’s Dave Portnoy, former Patriot star David Andrews, and content creator Emma Pritchard.
In Episode 2, she disclosed which hungry football players have been especially appreciative of her baking skills.
“The O-line is the main recipient of my baked goods,” she said.

Ann Michael Maye said that the players will send her funny thank you notes for the sweet treats, with messages like, “We need to protect your husband so we need to keep on the extra weight,” and, “If you need a bodyguard, we’ll be your bodyguard.”
The famous cookie that’s been fueling the Patriots ahead of Super Bowl weekend is Ann Michael’s “Cinnamon Roll Snickerdoodles,” which Drake Maye has claimed to be his favorite.
Fans Absolutely Love Her
Drake Maye is not the only one obsessed with his wife, Ann Michael Maye is adored by the Patroit’s fandom. She’s been dubbed “Queen of the North” and “First Lady of New England.”
“I’m only watching the Super Bowl because Ann is watching the Super Bowl,” one fan commented on her TikTok.
Fans expressed why they can’t get enough of Ann Michael in a TODAY segment that aired Feb. 6.
“I just think she’s so adorable and so unapologetically herself,” Merrick Moore said.
“It’s kind of nice because she doesn’t make Drake her personality,” Madye Moore said.
In preparation for the big game on Sunday, there are even “I ❤️ Ann Michael” sweatshirts being sold.
Ann Michael Maye Cheers Drake Maye On in Style
Ann Michael Maye attends her husband’s games and isn’t shy about her fashion. She typically stunts in comfortable yet colorful and bold attire.
Most recently, she served at the AFC Championship game, wearing a retro collaged Patriot coat with bright red pants.
What She’s Packing for the Super Bowl
On Feb. 4, Ann Michael Maye posted a TikTok of what she’s packing to the Super Bowl, and it includes three suitcases.
She added a luggage tag that says, “Go Pats 10,” referencing her husband’s number.

In her carryon, she had needlepoint, a “Harry Potter” book, a travel-sized mahjong set, a devotion book and her journal and of course a Patriots Hat. On the way, she finished a needlepoint luggage tag and also made one for her husband that says, “I love Drake Maye.”
It’s Ann Michael, Not Ann
Drake Maye pointed out that his wife’s full first name is Ann Michael, not Ann.
“Her name’s Ann Michael, not just Ann. I don’t like people calling her Ann and she doesn’t like it either,” he said in a press conference.
She Has ‘Beef’ with Seahawks’ QB’s Fiancée, Katie Hoofnagle
Ann Michael Maye and Katie Hoofnagle, who is engaged to Seahawks QB Sam Darnold, dropped ads for DoorDash joking about “settling the beef” ahead of the Super Bowl.
The beef, in this case, refers to which of their football teams’ home cities has better food.
Hoofnagle said, in hers, “no one does it better than Seattle,” calling ramen “kind of like clam chowder, but better.” Ann Michael Maye said New England’s coffee “dunks“ on Seattle’s.
The two posed in photos for the ads, showing the beef is all imaginary.

title: Here’s Who’s Performing the National Anthem at the Super Bowl 2026
summary: Who is singing the national anthem at the Super Bowl? What to know about all the performers and who sings the Star Spangled Banner.
story: The Super Bowl is full of fanfare, with a highly anticipated showdown on the field and an epic halftime performance.
Kicking it all off is a special rendition of the national anthem, often sung by a major celebrity.
Every year, one musician is given the opportunity to sing the Star-Spangled Banner in front of thousands of Super Bowl attendees and millions of viewers at home.
Curious to know who will be belting it out on the football field this year? Here are all the details.
Who Is Singing the 2026 Super Bowl National Anthem?
Musician Charlie Puth, who is known for hits like “We Don’t Talk Anymore” and “Attention,” will be singing the national anthem at the 2026 Super Bowl.
In an interview with Rolling Stone, Puth referred to the national anthem as “the hardest song ever written.”
The 34-year-old recalled how he ended up applying for the gig.
“I auditioned for it, but I made up my own audition because I’ve always wanted to do it ­— because I love it musically. It’s the best song. Musically, it’s so special,” he said.
When asked if he will be singing live, Puth said, “I’ll be singing. The mic will be on.” He also noted that certain parts of the song are prerecorded for practicality.
“It’s impossible to mic an orchestra and expect that it’s gonna sound good in a stadium that’s filled with a hundred thousand people that are going to be cheering,” he said.
During the interview, Puth noted that he’s inspired by Whitney Houston, who sang the national anthem at the Super Bowl in 1991 and is also a fellow New Jersey native.

“It’s a great honor. I’m going to be inspired by what Whitney did, but I can’t ever touch what she did. That’s the best one ever done — that and the Chris Stapleton one. That was raw. Made grown men cry. I just want to do my own thing with the hardest piece of music ever written. And I just wanna show people that I can do it,” he said.
Who’s Performing the Halftime Show?

Puerto Rican superstar Bad Bunny is taking the stage at the Apple Music Super Bowl 60 halftime show. This is the musician’s first time as a Super Bowl halftime show headliner, but he previously appeared as a guest in 2020 during Shakira and Jennifer Lopez’s 2020 halftime show performance.
Who Else is Performing?
In addition to the national anthem and halftime show, Super Bowl attendees and viewers will also be treated to several other performances.
Teddy Swims will kick things off by performing at the Super Bowl Tailgate at 3:50 p.m. ET. LaRussell will be his opening act. The performance will be available to stream live on Peacock.
At 6 p.m. ET, the following artists will perform during the Super Bowl Opening Ceremony: Green Day, Charlie Puth, Brandi Carlile and Coco Jones.

title: Nicolás Maduro is the 'legitimate president' of Venezuela, acting leader says
summary: Nicolás Maduro is still the legitimate leader of Venezuela, the country's acting president said in an exclusive interview with NBC News.“I can tell you President Nicolás Maduro is the legitimate president.
story: Nicolás Maduro is still the legitimate leader of Venezuela, the country's acting president said in an exclusive interview with NBC News.

“I can tell you President Nicolás Maduro is the legitimate president. I will tell you this as a lawyer, that I am. Both President Maduro and Cilia Flores, the first lady, are both innocent,” acting President Delcy Rodríguez told NBC News’ “Meet the Press” moderator Kristen Welker in Caracas, in her first interview with an American journalist since taking office.

As her predecessor sits in a federal detention facility in New York after being captured by U.S. forces last month, Rodríguez is now seemingly being welcomed back into the diplomatic fold by President Donald Trump. A high-profile visit Wednesday by the U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright came as the two countries hammered out details of how Venezuela’s vast oil reserves are to be distributed.

She added, “I can tell you I am in charge of the presidency of Venezuela, as it’s stated clearly in the constitution of Venezuela. And from the amount of work that I have, from how busy I am, I can tell you it’s very, very hard work and we’re doing it completely day by day.”
The daughter of a revolutionary and sanctioned by the first Trump administration, Rodríguez’s path to power has not been conventional.

In the days following Maduro’s capture on Jan. 3, Rodríguez was highly critical of the U.S. military action against him. She has since softened her tone and relented to U.S. pressure and demands, raising the possibility of a visit to the U.S. after two phone calls with Trump.
“I have been invited to the States,” Rodríguez told NBC News, adding that “we’re contemplating coming there once we establish this cooperation and we can move forward with everything.”

Still, the current government has not entirely shed the authoritarian ways of Maduro or his predecessor, the late President Hugo Chávez, who largely established the infrastructure of the current security state in Venezuela.
After being released from prison on Sunday, prominent opposition figure Juan Pablo Guanipa was rearrested and is currently under house arrest, according to a post on X by his son Ramon Guanipa.

“Ronald Reagan, trust but verify. Trust but verify. We’ve been dealing with Delcy for five weeks now. It’s been an amazing cooperation,” Secretary Wright told Welker in an exclusive interview in Caracas on Wednesday.

“She’s delivered information. Everything we know so far has turned out to be true. She’s made enormous positive changes, including already changing the hydrocarbon law in the country in the first few weeks. So I would say that cooperation is off to a tremendous start.”
